Indication: **Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)**

## Patient Summary

| Patient ID | Age | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Approx. Total Dose (mg) | Frequency | Next Dose | PK Clearance (mL/min) | Notes |

| CU-002 | 18 months | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 months | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

## Quick Assessment

- All three patients have active dosing schedules with defined individualized PK parameters.
- No acute instability flags are documented in the latest notes.
- CU-001 and CU-002 appear clinically stable/responding based on the latest annotations.
- CU-003 is newly enrolled, so closer near-term monitoring is appropriate given limited treatment history.

## PK Model Status Note

Based on the latest dosing configuration alone, the patient-specific PK parameters appear internally consistent and usable for current dosing review. However, this file does **not** include full model diagnostics or a fresh validation run, so this should be treated as a configuration-based stability check rather than a formal PK model performance review.", recipients="")]

From the latest config:

- CU-001: 0.15 mg/kg, 12.4 kg, ~1.86 mg per dose, q3w, next dose 2026-04-14
- CU-002: 0.12 mg/kg, 9.1 kg, ~1.09 mg per dose, q3w, next dose 2026-04-12
- CU-003: 0.18 mg/kg, 15.7 kg, ~2.83 mg per dose, q2w, next dose 2026-04-10

On PK stability: nothing in the latest dosing file suggests an immediate issue. The patient-specific parameters look consistent, and the notes indicate CU-001 and CU-002 are doing well. CU-003 looks fine from a config standpoint but is still early in treatment, so I'd treat that case as the one needing the closest follow-up.

If you want, I can also reformat this into a shorter physician-facing one-paragraph handoff.
